display.purpose: Landing identity and rare large album statements.

title.purpose: Feature headings, dialogs, and album section titles.

label.purpose: Buttons, compact controls, navigation, and metadata.

album-body.purpose: Narrative text printed inside album pages.

shadows.name: selection-lift
shadows.purpose: Selected segmented controls in dark mode.

shadows.name: page-lift
shadows.purpose: Album previews and landing-page imagery in dark mode.

shadows.name: overlay-lift
shadows.purpose: Menus and temporary overlays in dark mode.

motion.name: fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, and compact state feedback.

motion.name: normal
motion.purpose: Standard reveals and medium state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single emphasized action in a task area.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--q-primary, #0063d1); color: var(--text-on-color, #f0f0f5); min-height: 2.75rem; padding: .5rem .75rem; border: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md, .5rem); font: 500 .875rem/1.3 var(--font-ui, system-ui); cursor: pointer; transition: background .15s, transform .15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0558b8; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: .125rem solid var(--q-primary, #0063d1); outline-offset: .125rem; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(.98); }

components.name: Outlined Action
components.description: A secondary editor action with a reversible visual weight.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: transparent; color: var(--q-primary, #0063d1); min-height: 2.75rem; padding: .25rem .75rem; border: 1px solid var(--q-primary, #0063d1); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, .375rem); font: 500 .875rem/1.3 var(--font-ui, system-ui); cursor: pointer; transition: background .15s, color .15s; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--q-primary, #0063d1); color: var(--bg, #f3f4f6); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: .125rem solid var(--q-primary, #0063d1); outline-offset: .125rem; }

components.name: Outlined Field
components.description: A familiar text field for album and account properties.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: .25rem; color: var(--text-muted, #5d646f); font: 500 .75rem/1.3 var(--font-ui, system-ui); } .ds-field input { min-height: 2.75rem; padding: .5rem .75rem; color: var(--text, #1f2937); background: var(--bg-secondary, #fff); border: 1px solid var(--border-color, #d1d5db); border-radius: var(--radius-sm, .375rem); font: 400 1rem/1.5 var(--font-ui, system-ui); } .ds-field input:hover { border-color: var(--text-faint, #9ca3af); } .ds-field input:focus-visible { outline: 1px solid var(--q-primary, #0063d1); outline-offset: 0; border-color: var(--q-primary, #0063d1); }

components.name: Segmented Control
components.description: A compact mutually exclusive choice for appearance and units.
components.css: .ds-segmented { display: flex; gap: .125rem; padding: .125rem; border-radius: var(--radius-md, .5rem); background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--text, #1f2937) 8%, var(--bg-secondary, #fff)); } .ds-segmented button { flex: 1; padding: .375rem .75rem; border: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-sm, .375rem); background: transparent; color: var(--text-muted, #5d646f); font: 500 .875rem/1.3 var(--font-ui, system-ui); cursor: pointer; } .ds-segmented button[aria-pressed="true"] { background: var(--bg-secondary, #fff); color: var(--text-bright, #111827); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm, 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,.08)); } .ds-segmented button:focus-visible { outline: .125rem solid var(--q-primary, #0063d1); outline-offset: 1px; }

components.name: Workspace Card
components.description: A bordered task container with no resting shadow.
components.html:
Get your data from Polarsteps

Upload your export to build the first album draft.
components.css: .ds-card { max-width: 28rem; padding: 1.75rem; color: var(--text, #1f2937); background: var(--bg-secondary, #fff); border: 1px solid var(--border-color, #d1d5db); border-radius: var(--radius-xl, 1rem); font-family: var(--font-ui, system-ui); } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0 0 .5rem; color: var(--text-bright, #111827); font-size: 1.1rem; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; color: var(--text-muted, #5d646f); line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Editor Header
components.description: A shallow persistent header that leaves vertical space to the album.
components.css: .ds-editor-header { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: .75rem; padding: .5rem .75rem; color: var(--text-bright, #f0f0f5); background: var(--bg-secondary, #252540); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-color, #3a3a50); font: 400 1rem/1.3 var(--font-ui, system-ui); } .ds-editor-header strong { letter-spacing: -.02em; } .ds-editor-header span { color: var(--text-muted, #9ca3af); font-size: .875rem; } .ds-editor-header button { padding: .375rem .5rem; color: var(--text-muted, #9ca3af); background: var(--surface, #2a2a3e); border: 1px solid var(--border-color, #3a3a50); border-radius: 999px; } .ds-editor-header button:hover { color: var(--text-bright, #f0f0f5); } .ds-editor-header button:focus-visible { outline: .125rem solid var(--q-primary, #0063d1); outline-offset: .125rem; }

narrative.overview:
Wanderbound feels like a calm workspace built around a physical result. The interface is cool-toned, precise, and dependable, but the user's photographs, maps, and travel story supply the emotion. Product chrome stays restrained so the album remains the most visually important object on screen.

The system combines compact application controls with generous album surfaces. It supports light and dark themes without changing the hierarchy, uses motion for feedback and orientation, and treats the browser preview as an honest representation of the printed page. It rejects both decorative scrapbook software and dense professional design tools.
keyCharacteristics: Cool neutral work surfaces with one clear blue action color.
Compact, familiar controls surrounding large photographic page previews.
Precise print geometry with A4 landscape pages and millimeter-based gaps.
Responsive state feedback without decorative choreography.
Full English, Hebrew, RTL, light-theme, and dark-theme support.

rules.name: The Album Leads Rule
rules.body: If application chrome competes with a photograph or map, reduce the chrome.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Atlas Blue identifies interaction. Do not spend it on decoration.

rules.name: The Theme Parity Rule
rules.body: Light and dark themes may change values, never meaning, hierarchy, or available functionality.

rules.name: The Two Rooms Rule
rules.body: Assistant owns the application. Frank Ruhl Libre is reserved for album narrative content.

rules.name: The Earned Elevation Rule
rules.body: Persistent cards use a border or a tonal shift. Only pages, selected controls, and temporary overlays earn a shadow.

dos: Do make the album page, photograph, or map the dominant object.
Do use Atlas Blue for primary actions, current selection, progress, and focus.
Do keep application controls in Assistant and album narrative text in Frank Ruhl Libre.
Do preserve identical album visuals between preview and print.
Do preserve control meaning and hierarchy across light, dark, LTR, and RTL modes.
Do provide visible focus and reduced-motion behavior for core workflows.
donts: Don't make Wanderbound resemble a scrapbook editor.
Don't make Wanderbound resemble a template marketplace.
Don't make Wanderbound resemble a playful travel app filled with stickers and decorative effects.
Don't make Wanderbound resemble dense professional design software that exposes every possible control.
Don't use Binding Violet as a competing action color.
Don't add shadows to persistent cards merely for decoration.
Don't alter album content, spacing, or pagination only for print mode.
